title: Venezuela beats U.S. 3-2, wins first World Baseball Classic title
text: Venezuela won the World Baseball Classic for the first time, rebounding from a blown eighth-inning lead to beat the United States 3-2 Tuesday night on Eugenio Suárez's tiebreaking double in the ninth

title: Daniel Biss wins Democratic primary for closely-watched Illinois House seat
text: Daniel Biss wins Democratic primary for closely-watched Illinois House seat. Biss, the mayor of Evanston, Ill., topped political newcomer Kat Abughazaleh, a first-time candidate who ran as an unapologetic progressive in the race to succeed longtime incumbent Jan Schakowsky.

title: Arizona AG files criminal charges against Kalshi over 'illegal gambling'
text: Arizona is the first state to allege the prediction market company has committed criminal violations, accusing it of running an unlicensed gambling operation.

title: EU offers to pay Ukraine to fix oil pipeline at the center of Ukraine-Hungary feud
text: The European Union has offered financial and technical assistance to Ukraine to repair a critical oil pipeline serving Central Europe, aiming to resolve a diplomatic impasse with Hungary. The EU hopes this intervention will convince Hungary to withdraw its veto on substantial aid packages destined for Ukraine, indicating that infrastructure disputes are complicating European unity on Ukraine support.

title: Judge orders 1,000 Voice of America staffers back to work in rebuke to Kari Lake
text: A federal judge has mandated the return of over 1,000 Voice of America employees to work by Monday, delivering a significant legal setback to the Trump administration's efforts to drastically downsize the U.S. government-funded international broadcaster. The ruling represents a judicial rebuke to Kari Lake's leadership and constrains the administration's ability to restructure the outlet, which serves as America's official external broadcasting service reaching global audiences.

title: Top US counterterrorism official resigns over Iran war, urging Trump to 'reverse course'
text: National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent has resigned in protest over U.S. military action against Iran, publicly urging President Trump to reverse course. Kent directly attributed the conflict's initiation to pressure from Israel and pro-Israel lobbying groups in the United States. This represents an extraordinary public dissent from a senior intelligence official during an active military engagement, potentially signaling deeper divisions within the national security establishment regarding the Iran conflict and raising questions about the administration's decision-making process and domestic political influences on military policy.

title: Death of Ali Larijani deepens crisis at heart of Iran's leadership
text: Ali Larijani, one of Iran's most experienced and influential policymakers, has died, intensifying an ongoing leadership crisis within the Islamic Republic. Larijani's death removes a key figure from Iran's political establishment at a time when the regime faces significant internal challenges. His decades of experience in senior positions, including as Speaker of Parliament and Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, made him a central player in Iranian foreign and domestic policy formulation. The loss creates a vacuum in leadership continuity and institutional knowledge during a period of heightened regional tensions and domestic instability.

title: Actor Sean Penn gifted a special ‘Oscar’ by Ukraine’s railway company
text: Sean Penn was gifted an 'Oscar' made of Ukrainian railway metal after he skipped the Oscars to visit war-torn Ukraine.

title: Total repression and air strikes bring unrelenting dread for Iranians
text: Tehran residents report being trapped between escalating US-Israeli air strikes and intensified domestic repression as the Iranian regime attempts to consolidate power. The dual pressures of external military bombardment and internal authoritarian crackdown are creating severe psychological and physical strain on the civilian population, indicating a dangerous convergence of external conflict and internal instability that could have significant regional implications.

title: Spanish king reopens debate on conquest of Mexico by acknowledging 'abuse'
text: King Felipe of Spain has acknowledged abuses during the Spanish conquest of Mexico approximately 500 years ago, a statement that appears to improve strained diplomatic relations between Spain and Mexico while simultaneously reigniting historical debates about colonial-era actions. The acknowledgment represents a significant shift in Spain's official position on its colonial legacy and may have broader implications for Spain-Latin America relations, though it also risks domestic political controversy over how Spain addresses its imperial past.

title: Air strike hit Kabul rehab centre as patients ate dinner, survivor tells BBC
text: A Pakistani air strike hit a rehabilitation center in Kabul during dinner on Monday night, with casualties feared to number in the hundreds, though exact figures remain unconfirmed. A survivor provided testimony to BBC describing the attack on the civilian medical facility. This represents a major escalation in cross-border military action and a significant mass-casualty incident targeting non-combatants receiving medical care.

title: Cuba restores power after 29-hour blackout amid US oil blockade
text: Cuba has restored its national power grid after a 29-hour blackout that left all 10 million residents without electricity. The extended outage occurred amid ongoing US oil sanctions that have severely constrained Cuba's fuel supply and energy infrastructure. The incident highlights Cuba's continuing vulnerability to power grid failures and the humanitarian impact of sustained economic pressure on critical infrastructure serving the entire population.

title: Trump confirms meeting with China's Xi Jinping delayed as war on Iran rages
text: US President Donald Trump has postponed his scheduled trip to Beijing for a meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping as the United States is engaged in active military conflict with Iran. Concurrently, Trump is seeking China's assistance to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil chokepoint. The delay signals how the Iran conflict is disrupting major diplomatic initiatives and forcing the US to manage multiple strategic priorities simultaneously, including enlisting Chinese cooperation on Middle East maritime security while bilateral tensions persist.

title: Trump administration defends Anthropic blacklisting in US court
text: The Trump administration is defending in federal court its decision to designate AI company Anthropic as a supply chain risk, a blacklisting triggered by the company's refusal to remove safety guardrails from its artificial intelligence technology. The US Defense Secretary invoked supply chain security authorities to justify the designation, marking an unprecedented confrontation between the federal government and a major AI developer over content moderation and safety controls. This case represents a significant escalation in tensions between tech companies maintaining ethical AI standards and government demands for unrestricted access to AI capabilities, with potential implications for the broader AI industry's relationship with federal authorities and the future of AI safety protocols in commercial products used by government agencies.

title: Araghchi: Iran's system holds despite targeted leaders
text: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi has responded publicly to recent assassinations of senior Iranian officials, asserting that Iran's governmental system remains stable and functional despite these targeted killings. The statement appears designed to project resilience and continuity of leadership amid what Iran characterizes as external attacks on its command structure, though specific details about which officials were killed or the perpetrators are not provided in this brief report.
